CSCE 445 Syllabus

Section 550 (59023)

Computers and New Media

Spring 2026 - College Station

Course Information

End Time: 3:35PM

Start Date: 01/12/2026

End Date: 05/05/2026

Meeting Location:  CLB 114

Credit Hours:  3

Instructor Details

Yanlai Wu

Email:  yanlaiwu@tamu.edu

Office Location:  ECRB 309C

Phone:  409-740-5514

Office Hours

TR 12:00pm-1:00pm

Catalog Description

Computers and New Media. (3-0). Credit 3. Potential and realized impact of

computers in the design of new media; relationship between authors and readers

of interactive material; influence of media design on the content expressed.

Prerequisite: CSCE 221 or approval of instructor.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  CSCE 221 or approval of instructor.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

analyze characteristics of computer-based media and anticipate likely reactions to

them

design software features that support immersion, agency, and transformation

describe techniques for creating computer characters and narrative control

develop software for the manipulation/use of audio and/or video

describe core differences between physical and intellectual property

assess characteristics of software licenses, software patents, and digital rights

management software

design, implement and evaluate software supporting the creation and/or

consumption of content in specific settings

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

Short assignments  (20%) : There will be five assignments due in class throughout

the semester. These assignments may require use of specific software outside of

class time and will take the form of short essays, written answers to questions, and

design documents. All material turned in should be printed using a computer

printer or typed except when noted otherwise.

Write-ups  (20%) : There will be four write-ups throughout the semester. Each write-

up should summarize and reflect on the assigned readings from both the current

week and the previous week. All write-ups are due before class begins on the

designated date and must be submitted on time for full credit.

Topic presentation  (20%) : Each student will select one topic from the course

schedule to present. Presentation dates will be scheduled after all students have

signed up for their topics.

Quizzes  (10%) : Quizzes will be prepared and administered by the student

presenters. Each presenter is required to prepare 10 quiz questions based on their

selected chapter or research paper, ensuring the questions reflect the key concepts

and arguments covered in their presentation.

Final project  (30%) : The final project will consist of three components: a short in-

class presentation, a written final report, and a prototype related to the topic or

problem the team chooses to explore. Students may complete the final project

individually or in a team of 2–3 members.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Late work will not be accepted in the class, except for students with an

excused absence. If students have difficulties finishing an assignment,

contact the instructor  before  the deadline.

AI Statement

Generative AI Policy

Writing:  Use of AI tools, including ChatGPT, is permitted in this course for

students who wish to use them. To adhere to our scholarly values, students

must cite any AI-generated material that informed their work (this includes in-

text citations and/or use of quotations, and in your reference list). Using an

AI tool to generate content without proper attribution qualifies as academic

dishonesty. See  www.chicagomanualofstyle.org   for information how to

properly cite AI tools.

(adapted from University of Massachusetts Amherst)
